response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer, but his profile does not match this role's mandatory requirements. His C++ experience is limited to a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a thesis project, far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. His recent decade centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms. He has brief fintech exposure at RIDE Capital, but no capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking systems experience, and his database background (MySQL, MongoDB) does not cover SQL Server or Oracle.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of requests per second)
Solid CI/CD, DevOps, and testing background, plus active open-source contributions and teaching
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months in 2014-2015), versus the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain experience; RIDE Capital was Node/TypeScript fintech
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; no Summit platform exposure

key_factors.factor: Missing capital markets / fixed income domain expertise
Strong general engineering and performance-systems background
